flashcards.front: What is the difference between Computer Architecture and Computer Organization?
flashcards.back: Computer Architecture refers to the abstract design of a computer system including its instruction set, addressing modes, and data types. Computer Organization is the actual hardware implementation - how the components are interconnected and operate together.

flashcards.front: What are the three main components of a computer system?
flashcards.back: The three main components are: 1) Central Processing Unit (CPU) - processes data, 2) Memory - stores data and instructions, 3) Input/Output (I/O) - interface with external devices.

The fundamental operation cycle of the CPU where it: 1) Fetches an instruction from memory, 2) Decodes the instruction to determine what operation to perform, 3) Executes the operation using appropriate hardware components.

flashcards.front: What is the role of the Control Unit (CU) in the CPU?
flashcards.back: The Control Unit directs the operation of the processor by generating control signals that coordinate the movement of data between CPU components, memory, and I/O devices during instruction execution.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between RAM and ROM?
flashcards.back: RAM (Random Access Memory) is volatile memory that can be read from and written to, used for temporary storage. ROM (Read-Only Memory) is non-volatile and contains permanent instructions that cannot be modified by normal operations.

flashcards.front: What is a bus in computer architecture?
flashcards.back: A bus is a communication pathway that connects different components of a computer system, allowing data transfer between the CPU, memory, and I/O devices. Common types include data bus, address bus, and control bus.

An instruction set is the collection of all machine instructions that a CPU can execute, including arithmetic, logical, data transfer, control, and I/O instructions. It defines the interface between software and hardware.

flashcards.front: What is the function of the Arithmetic Logic Unit (ALU)?
flashcards.back: The ALU performs arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) and logical operations (AND, OR, NOT, comparison) on binary data as directed by the control unit.

The memory hierarchy is a tiered structure of storage devices organized by speed and size: Registers (fastest), Cache, Main Memory (RAM), Secondary Storage (hard disk), and Tertiary Storage (tapes). Each level trades speed for capacity.

flashcards.front: What is a register in the CPU?
flashcards.back: Registers are small, high-speed storage locations within the CPU used to hold data, addresses, and control information during processing. Examples include Program Counter (PC), Instruction Register (IR), and Accumulator (ACC).

flashcards.front: What is the Program Counter (PC) register?
flashcards.back: The Program Counter holds the address of the next instruction to be fetched from memory. After each instruction fetch, it is automatically incremented (or modified by branch/jump instructions) to point to the next instruction.

The Instruction Register temporarily stores the current instruction being executed. It holds the opcode and operand information while the control unit decodes the instruction to determine what operation to perform.

flashcards.front: What is the difference between a byte and a word?
flashcards.back: A byte is the basic unit of computer memory, typically 8 bits. A word is the natural unit of data used by a particular processor architecture, commonly 16, 32, or 64 bits depending on the CPU design.

An addressing mode defines how the operand of an instruction is specified. Common modes include: Immediate (constant value), Direct (address given), Indirect (address points to address), Register, and Indexed addressing.

flashcards.front: What is the function of the system clock?
flashcards.back: The system clock generates regular timing pulses that synchronize all computer operations. It controls the speed at which instructions are executed and ensures proper coordination between different components of the CPU and memory.
